|SUBJECT: LANDING GEAR - MAIN LANDING GEAR COMPLETE - INTRODUCTION OF A NEW CAP - GROUP , CONFIG. 1

1.00| | | |Weight & Balance:

1. There will be an increase in weight of 0,030 kg (0.0661 lb) for each main landing gear. A total increase in weight for the aircraft of 0,060 kg (0.1322 lb).| | |Electrical Load Data:

1. Not changed.| | | |Operational Impact / Caution :| | | | | | |Revision Comment :| | | | | | |Description :

1. Safran Landing Systems tells operators that a new cap is introduced to prevent contact between the 1M strut harness and a bolt in the location of the sliding piston fork.
2. Safran Landing Systems recommend that you do this Service Bulletin on main landing gears on which:


- the strike number 10 has not been cancelled on the 10-357303 LH Series and 10-357403 RH Series shock strut dressings nameplate and looks like this 10 .

| | |---| |MATERIAL REQUIREMENT| |The I/D codes below give information which help you to understand the instruction/disposition. I/D Code Instruction/information

A New part
B Part that can be obtained from the old one
C Expendable part
D Old part to be discarded
E Part of new design valid for new manufacture
F Part number change only


Interchangeability codes (IC) given below show basic information to help you understand the interchangeability of parts. For full explanation of interchangeability codes, please refer to the latest edition of ATA specification 2000.

IC Instruction/information

01 One-way interchangeable
02 Two-way interchangeable
03 Not interchangeable
04 Interchangeable as a set
05 Qualified Interchangeability


The table that follows tells operators of the items that are necessary to do this Service Bulletin. It does not include tools or materials that are referred to in the Component Maintenance Manuals. Prices shown in the table that follows are for a quantity 1 of each part.

SERVICE BULLETIN| | | |TOOL REQUIREMENT| |None| | | |REFERENCES / GENERAL COMMENT / NOTES / CAUTIONS| |References:|

|1. Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Component Maintenance Manual 32-13-06.
2. Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Modification Form No. EDES2-0037-0337, issue 1.
3. This Service Bulletin refers to Process Specifications (M-DLPS, M-DLNDT and PCS): these are available in the Safran Landing Systems Technical Publications on-line service.
4. This Service Bulletin is subject to aircraft modification No. 112981 L04001 (classified minor) and is covered by aircraft Service Bulletin A350-32-P031. Under no circumstances should the modified equipment, resulting from the application of this SB, be installed on the aircraft type unless its aircraft SB is approved.


1. Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Component Maintenance Manual 32-13-02.
2. Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Component Maintenance Manual 32-13-06.

GENERAL COMMENT / NOTES / CAUTIONS:

GROUP , Configuration 1|

|ACKNOWLEDGEMENT

Prior proceeding with Accomplishment Instructions|Certifying Staff| |---|---| | |Sign/Stamp/Date| |1. All Materials / Tools / Kits related to the accomplishment of this EI, has been reviewed and verified for availability prior proceeding with the accomplishment of the EI.| | |2. Signing of IIR/RII

E.g: Tasks/Steps identified in the EI requiring Independent Inspection:| | |![image 6]()| | |3. All certifying engineers/staff involved in the accomplishment of this EI must independently review the EI and the subsequent related tasks and subtasks called by higher level tasks prior to accomplishment, ensuring to identify any further Independent Inspection Requirement tasks/steps and follow EYEng TPM 4-43 or customer EO/WO/Task Card requirements.| |

|The same person shall not carry out and inspect work, in relation to a maintenance task involving some element of disassembly/reassembly of several aircraft components of the same type, fitted to more than one system on the same aircraft during a particular maintenance check.

NOTE: Refer to TPM 4-43 for Independent Inspection Requirements.| |---|

|Accomplishment Instructions:|MECH|INSP|Independent Inspection (II)|
|---|---|---|---|
| |Sign/Staff No/ Date|Sign/Stamp/ Date|Sign/Stamp/Date/ Time|

|General| | | | |---|---|---|---| |1. Not applicable.| | | | |Preparation| | | | |1. Refer to Airbus Service Bulletin A350-32-P031.| | | | |Procedure| | | | |1. Procedure (refer to CMM 32-13-06 and Figures 1 to 3 of this Service Bulletin).

1. Remove the cotter pins (2-10).
2. At the applicable assembly step of the shock strut dressings: 1 Install the new lower cap 55-3573319-00 on the nuts (2-20). 2 Install the cotter pins (2-10) with their heads parallel to the bolt (2-40) head. Make sure that the heads of the cotter pins are hard against castellations of the nuts (2-20). Bend the legs of the cotter pins laterally around the nut castellations to safety them: refer to PCS-7610. NOTE: The cotter pins (2-10) are used to keep the lower cap 55-3573319-00 in position.
3 Apply sealant to the nuts (2-20) and the cotter pins (2-10): refer to PCS-7200.
4 Install the new upper cap 55-3573317-00 on the lower cap 55-3573319-00.
5 Install the new cotter pins MS24665-155 through the upper cap 55-3573317-00 and the lower cap 55-3573319-00 and safety them: refer to PCS-7610.
6 Complete the assembly of the shock strut dressings: refer to ASSEMBLY in the CMM. SERVICE BULLETIN
| | | | |Re-Identification of equipment

NOTE: When it is necessary to replace the identification nameplate identify the replacement nameplate with all the applicable data.| | | | |1. After you have done this Service Bulletin:

- cancel the related strike number 10 on the 10-357303 LH Series and 10-357403 RH Series shock strut dressings nameplate, refer to M-DLPS1006-5 and cancel like this 10
- write the Service Bulletin number 10-355-32-29 in the applicable aircraft and landing gear records.
| | | | |Test| | | |

|1. Not applicable.| | | |
